steps.label: Applicant Intake and Eligibility Workflow
steps.description: This ScreenQueue guides the applicant through a multi-step intake and eligibility workflow, including personal information entry, household size, income details, citizenship status, and final review/confirmation before submission.

sequenceLogic.skipLogic: Screens may be skipped if user is not eligible or workflow is cancelled. If 'Non-Citizen' or 'Other' is selected for citizenship, additional documentation screens may be triggered outside this queue.

quick_action_buttons.label: Print
quick_action_buttons.description: Prints the eligibility summary for the applicant, excluding attachments. Useful for record-keeping or offline review.

quick_action_buttons.label: Export PDF
quick_action_buttons.description: Exports the entire application workflow and entered data as a PDF document for download or sharing.

screens.label: Applicant Personal Information Entry
screens.description: On this screen, you will enter your personal details to begin your application. Please provide accurate information as it will be used to identify you and process your application. Fields marked with an asterisk (*) are required. You will see clear instructions for each field, and guidance is provided to help you complete the form correctly.
data_validation: Date of Birth cannot be in the future.
Phone number must be in valid format.
Email must be in valid format.

screens.label: Household Size Information
screens.description: On this screen, you will enter the number of people living in your household. This information is important for determining eligibility and benefit amounts. Please count all individuals who live with you, including yourself. If you need help, refer to the guidance provided on the screen.
data_validation: Household Size must be a positive integer.

screens.label: Income Details Entry
screens.description: On this screen, you will provide details about your household's income. Please enter all sources of income for each household member. You can add multiple income entries if needed. Accurate income information is required for eligibility determination. Guidance is provided for each field, and you can add or remove income entries as needed.
data_validation: Income Amount must be a positive number.
Cannot have duplicate income entries for the same type and recipient.
Income Type, Amount, Frequency, and Recipient are required for each entry.

buttons.label: Add Income Entry
buttons.description: Add another income source for your household.

screens.label: Citizenship Status Selection
screens.description: On this screen, you will select your citizenship status. This information is required for eligibility purposes. Please choose the option that best describes your current status. If you are not a U.S. citizen, you may be asked to provide additional documentation later in the process.
data_validation: If Non-Citizen or Other is selected, additional documentation may be required.

screens.label: Application Review and Confirmation
screens.description: On this screen, you will review all the information you have entered so far. Please carefully check each section for accuracy. If you need to make changes, you can go back to the relevant section. Once you are satisfied, you can submit your application. A summary of your personal information, household details, income, and citizenship status will be displayed for your confirmation.
data_validation: All required fields must be completed before submission.
If any required field is missing, user must return to the relevant section for correction.
Income entries must be valid and non-negative.
Date of Birth cannot be in the future.
Household Size must be a positive integer.

actions.name: Validate Personal Information Fields
actions.implementation: Validates required fields (Full Name, Date of Birth, Address) and business rules (Date of Birth not in future) on the Personal Information screen. If validation passes, proceed to Household Size screen. If validation fails, display error messages inline, highlight invalid fields, and prevent navigation.

actions.name: Validate Household Size Field
actions.implementation: Validates required field (Household Size) and business rules (must be positive integer, not zero) on the Household Size screen. If validation passes, proceed to Income Details screen. If validation fails, display error messages inline, highlight invalid fields, and prevent navigation.

actions.name: Validate Income Entry
actions.implementation: Validates required fields (Income Type, Income Amount, Frequency, Income Recipient) and business rules (Income Amount must be positive, no duplicate entries) when adding a new income entry. If validation passes, add entry and display success toast. If validation fails, display error messages inline, highlight invalid fields, and prevent addition.
actions.triggerCondition: Applicant clicks 'Add Income Entry' on STEP_1_3

actions.name: Validate Income Details Fields
actions.implementation: Validates all required income fields and business rules (positive amount, no duplicates) before allowing navigation to Citizenship Status screen. If validation passes, proceed. If validation fails, display error messages inline, highlight invalid fields, and prevent navigation.

actions.name: Validate Citizenship Status Field
actions.implementation: Validates required field (Citizenship Status) on Citizenship Status screen. If Non-Citizen or Other is selected, trigger additional documentation workflow. If validation passes, proceed to Application Review screen. If validation fails, display error messages inline, highlight invalid fields, and prevent navigation.

actions.name: Validate Application Review Fields
actions.implementation: Validates that all required fields (Full Name, Date of Birth, Address, Household Size, Income Details, Citizenship Status) are completed and valid before allowing submission. If validation passes, proceed to submission. If validation fails, display summary error messages, highlight missing/invalid fields, and prevent submission.

actions.name: Navigate Back to Previous Screen
actions.implementation: Allows applicant to return to the previous intake screen for corrections. Navigation is only allowed if the previous screen exists. After navigation, display the previous screen and retain entered data.
actions.triggerCondition: Applicant clicks 'Back' on any intake screen (STEP_1_2, STEP_1_3, STEP_1_4, STEP_1_5)

actions.name: Navigate to Next Screen After Review
actions.implementation: After successful validation on Application Review screen, navigates applicant to submission workflow (STEP_3).
actions.triggerCondition: Applicant clicks 'Next' on STEP_1_5 and validation passes

actions.name: Open Help Modal
actions.implementation: Opens a modal window with help documentation relevant to the current screen. Content source is determined by the screen context.

actions.name: Open Settings Modal
actions.implementation: Opens a modal window for user settings. Allows applicant to view and edit settings.

actions.name: Open User Profile Modal
actions.implementation: Opens a modal window for user profile. Allows applicant to view and edit profile information.
actions.triggerCondition: Applicant clicks 'User Profile' on any screen

actions.name: Print Eligibility Summary
actions.implementation: Triggers print dialog for eligibility summary, excluding attachments. If print fails, display error toast and allow retry.
actions.triggerCondition: Applicant clicks 'Print' quick action button on STEP_1_5

actions.name: Export Application as PDF
actions.implementation: Exports the entire application workflow and entered data as a PDF document for download or sharing. If export fails, display error toast and allow retry.
actions.triggerCondition: Applicant clicks 'Export PDF' quick action button on STEP_1_5

steps.label: Check Required Fields Completion
steps.description: This rule verifies that all required fields (Full Name, Date of Birth, Address, Household Size, Income Details, and Citizenship Status) are completed before allowing the applicant to submit the application. If any required field is missing, the system prevents submission and returns the applicant to the Review Application screen for correction.

rules.label: All Required Fields Completed
rules.logic: If Full Name, Date of Birth, Address, Household Size, Income Details, and Citizenship Status are all present and not empty, allow submission to proceed.

rules.label: Missing Required Fields
rules.logic: If any of Full Name, Date of Birth, Address, Household Size, Income Details, or Citizenship Status is missing or empty, prevent submission and return to Review Application for correction.

actions.name: Check Required Fields Completion
actions.implementation: Checks if all required fields (Full Name, Date of Birth, Address, Household Size, Income Details, Citizenship Status) are present and not empty. If all are present, proceed to submission (STEP_3). If any are missing, prevent submission, display error summary at top of STEP_1_5, highlight missing/invalid fields, and navigate back to Application Review screen for correction.

actions.name: Navigate Back to Application Review for Correction
actions.implementation: If required fields are missing or invalid, system navigates applicant back to Application Review screen (STEP_1_5), highlights missing/invalid fields in red, displays error summary at top, and provides inline guidance for correction.

steps.label: Submit Application
steps.description: This rule governs the process of submitting the completed SNAP application. Upon clicking the 'Submit Application' button, the system saves the application data, generates a unique confirmation number, and displays a confirmation message to the applicant.

rules.label: Save Application Data
rules.logic: On submit, persist all entered application data (Full Name, Date of Birth, Address, Household Size, Income Details, Citizenship Status) to the application record.

rules.label: Generate Confirmation Number
rules.logic: After saving the application data, generate a unique confirmation number for the application.

rules.label: Display Confirmation Message
rules.logic: Display a confirmation message to the applicant including the confirmation number after successful submission.

actions.name: Persist Application Data
actions.implementation: Saves all entered application data (Full Name, Date of Birth, Address, Household Size, Income Details, Citizenship Status) to the application record. If persistence is successful, proceed to confirmation number generation. If persistence fails, display error message, log error, and allow applicant to retry submission.

actions.name: Check Data Persistence Success
actions.implementation: Checks if application data was successfully saved. If successful, proceed to generate confirmation number. If not, display error message, log error, and allow applicant to retry submission.

actions.name: Generate Confirmation Number
actions.implementation: Generates a unique confirmation number for the application after successful data persistence. If generation fails, display error message, log error, and allow retry.

actions.name: Display Confirmation Message
actions.implementation: Displays a confirmation message to the applicant including the confirmation number after successful submission. If display fails, log error, display error toast, and allow retry.

actions.name: Notify Applicant of Successful Submission
actions.implementation: Sends email and in-app notification to applicant confirming successful submission and providing confirmation number. If notification fails, log error and allow retry.

actions.name: Trigger Assignment to Caseworker
actions.implementation: After successful application submission and confirmation number generation, triggers assignment of the application to an available caseworker (STEP_4).

steps.label: Assign to Caseworker
steps.description: Automatically assign the submitted application to an available SNAP caseworker for review. The system should select a caseworker based on availability and workload balancing criteria, update the application record with the assigned caseworker's identifier, and trigger a notification to the caseworker for further processing.

assignments.label: Assign to Caseworker
assignments.logic: Upon successful submission of the application, the system queries the pool of SNAP caseworkers, selects one based on current workload and availability, and updates the application record with the assigned caseworker's ID. If an error occurs (e.g., database transaction error as indicated by the 'status' and 'message' fields), the system should log the error and retry the assignment or escalate to an administrator.

actions.name: Assign Application to Caseworker
actions.implementation: System queries the pool of SNAP caseworkers, selects one based on current workload and availability, updates the application record with the assigned caseworker's ID, and triggers notification to the caseworker. If assignment is successful, proceed to STEP_5. If assignment fails, trigger error handling and escalation.
actions.triggerCondition: Application submitted and confirmation number generated (ACTION_3_6)

actions.name: Handle Assignment Failure and Escalation
actions.implementation: If assignment to caseworker fails (e.g., due to database error or no available caseworker), log the error, retry assignment up to 2 times. If still unsuccessful, send notification to administrator via email and in-app, update application status to 'Assignment Pending', and notify applicant of delay with estimated resolution time.

steps.label: Review Assigned Application
steps.description: The caseworker must review the SNAP application assigned for processing, verify all required information, and initiate eligibility determination. This includes checking completeness, accuracy, and compliance with program requirements before proceeding to the next stage.
steps.taskName: Review SNAP Application for Eligibility Determination

completionConditions.logic: Caseworker must confirm that all required fields are present, validate the accuracy of applicant information, and document any discrepancies or missing data. Task is complete when the caseworker marks the application as reviewed and ready for eligibility determination.

actions.name: Notify Caseworker of Assignment
actions.implementation: Upon successful assignment, system triggers email and in-app notification to the assigned caseworker with application details and review due date. If notification fails, log error and allow retry.

actions.name: Review Assigned Application
actions.implementation: Caseworker reviews the assigned SNAP application, verifies all required information, checks completeness, accuracy, and compliance with program requirements. Discrepancies or missing data are documented in a review notes section. If review is complete and accurate, mark as reviewed. If discrepancies are found, return application to intake and specify which screens require correction.
actions.triggerCondition: Caseworker opens assigned application for review on STEP_5

actions.name: Mark Application as Reviewed
actions.implementation: Caseworker marks the application as reviewed and ready for eligibility determination after confirming all required fields and accuracy. System updates application status, logs review completion, and notifies applicant of review completion.
actions.triggerCondition: Caseworker clicks 'Mark as Reviewed' on STEP_5

actions.name: Return Application to Applicant Intake
actions.implementation: If discrepancies or missing data are found, caseworker returns the application to intake. System updates application status, notifies applicant of required corrections via email and in-app notification, and reopens relevant intake screens (STEP_1_1 to STEP_1_5) for editing. Review notes specify which sections require correction.
actions.triggerCondition: Caseworker clicks 'Return' during review on STEP_5

actions.name: Open Help Modal (Caseworker)
actions.implementation: Opens a modal window with help documentation relevant to the caseworker review screen. Content source is '/help/application-review'.
